statement.p: A triangles height is 2 feet longer than 4 \text{ times} its base. If we use b to represent the triangles base, use a polynomial to represent the triangles area in expanded form. A triangles area can be calculated by A=\frac{1}{2}bh, where b stands for base, and h stands for height.

solution.p: The triangles base is b feet. Since the triangles height is 2 feet longer than 4 \text{ times} its base, its height is 4b+2 feet. We substitute those two values into a triangles area formula, and we have:
The triangles area is {2b^{2}+b} square feet.
